subject: Grade 8 Cross-Curricular
grade_subject_fit: Reusable Grade 8 planning toolkit for Math 8, Science 8, ELA 8, Career Education 8, and ADST 8.
unit_context: Teacher-facing toolkit for building future packages from curriculum target, context family, student product, support version, and assessment evidence.
assignment_purpose: Teachers build a defended package plan where context, product, support version, and evidence all connect back to the selected curriculum target.
final_evidence_target: A package passoff that explains target, context, product, support version, assessment evidence, and separated teacher/student artifacts.
student_task_flow: Choose or receive a matching context.
Complete the done-when checklist and completion_check.
success_criteria: Teacher and student materials are separated.
supports_scaffolds: Use target cards to reduce planning drift.
Use K-support moves that preserve the learning goal.
Use the student support frame for reduced-load versions.

differentiation_model.support_pathway: Use a preselected target, one context family, and the student support frame.
differentiation_model.core_pathway: Choose target, context, product, support version, and assessment evidence.
differentiation_model.extension_pathway: Compare two context families and justify which better protects the target.

teacher_implementation_notes: This toolkit is for planning future packages.
Garden / planter / food systems is one option, not the default.
For First Peoples content, use teacher-selected/local-approved sources only.
likely_misconceptions: Context can become decoration if the target is not named first.
Student choice can hide the learning goal.
Support can accidentally lower the target instead of reducing barriers.

teacher_guide.title: Teacher Guide - Grade 8 Curriculum Target Card Toolkit
teacher_guide.overview: Build future Grade 8 packages from curriculum target + context + student product + support version + assessment evidence.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do we keep packages aligned to the target instead of driven by a favourite context?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Start with the curriculum target. Choose the context only after target and evidence are clear.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Build a Grade 8 package by choosing a curriculum target first, then selecting a context family, student product, support version, and assessment evidence.
materials: quick_tools: Do not default everything to garden or planter context.
quick_tools: Keep answer keys and teacher look-fors out of student-facing materials.
quick_tools: Every student packet needs done-when checklist or completion_check signal.

model.title: Teacher model
model.sample: Target: unit rates. Context: consumer/money. Product: best-buy recommendation. Support: partial table. Evidence: unit-rate comparison and explanation.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: assessment_look_fors: context supports target; product shows evidence; K-support preserves the goal; teacher/student materials are separated; artifact roles are clear.
teacher_notes: teacher_notes: Garden / planter / food systems is optional, not default.
teacher_notes: Use local-approved sources for First Peoples content.

toolkit_overview.title: Toolkit Overview
toolkit_overview.overview: Teacher-facing overview for using target cards before fixture creation.
toolkit_overview.project_prompt: Use the chain: curriculum target + context + student product + support version + assessment evidence.
materials: Garden / planter / food systems is one optional context family only.
Use the artifact checklist before rendering.
toolkit_overview.assessment_focus: Look for clear target, observable evidence, context alignment, and teacher/student separation.
teacher_notes: Do not copy teacher planning notes into student pages.

context_menu.title: Context Menu
context_menu.overview: Teacher-facing menu for package context families.
context_menu.project_prompt: Choose a context family only after the curriculum target is clear.
context_menu.assessment_focus: Context must support the learning goal and not replace the curriculum target.
teacher_notes: Garden / planter / food systems is optional, not default.

blank_curriculum_target_card_template.title: Blank Curriculum Target Card Template
blank_curriculum_target_card_template.overview: Teacher-facing template for planning one package target.
blank_curriculum_target_card_template.project_prompt: Complete target, student-friendly goal, best evidence, common difficulties, K-support moves, possible contexts, and likely artifacts.
blank_curriculum_target_card_template.assessment_focus: Target is clear, evidence is observable, and support preserves the learning goal.
teacher_notes: Complete one card before building artifacts.

math8_target_card_set.title: Math 8 Target Card Set
math8_target_card_set.overview: Teacher-facing compact Math 8 index. Full bank is in docs/curriculum-target-cards/math8.md.
math8_target_card_set.project_prompt: Choose one Math 8 target before context or artifact production.
math8_target_card_set.assessment_focus: Evidence must show calculation, representation, reasoning, or interpretation tied to the target.
teacher_notes: Use practical contexts without burying math in long reading.

science8_target_card_set.title: Science 8 Target Card Set
science8_target_card_set.overview: Teacher-facing compact Science 8 index. Full bank is in docs/curriculum-target-cards/science8.md.
science8_target_card_set.project_prompt: Choose one Science 8 target before context or artifact production.
science8_target_card_set.assessment_focus: Evidence must show observation, model, explanation, diagram, data, or CER tied to the target.
teacher_notes: Use visuals and vocabulary supports when reading load is high.

ela8_target_card_set.title: ELA 8 Target Card Set
ela8_target_card_set.overview: Teacher-facing compact ELA 8 index. Full bank is in docs/curriculum-target-cards/ela8.md.
ela8_target_card_set.project_prompt: Choose one literacy target before context or artifact production.
ela8_target_card_set.assessment_focus: Evidence must show reading, writing, speaking, representing, or response tied to the target.
teacher_notes: Do not invent generic First Peoples stories or knowledge.

careers8_target_card_set.title: Career Education 8 Target Card Set
careers8_target_card_set.overview: Teacher-facing compact Careers 8 index. Full bank is in docs/curriculum-target-cards/careers8.md.
careers8_target_card_set.project_prompt: Choose one Careers 8 target before context or artifact production.
careers8_target_card_set.assessment_focus: Evidence must show reflection, planning, communication, pathway thinking, or skill connection tied to the target.

adst8_target_card_set.title: ADST 8 Target Card Set
adst8_target_card_set.overview: Teacher-facing compact ADST 8 index. Full bank is in docs/curriculum-target-cards/adst8.md.
adst8_target_card_set.project_prompt: Choose one ADST 8 target before context or artifact production.
adst8_target_card_set.assessment_focus: Evidence must show planning, making, testing, safety, feedback, or reflection tied to the target.
teacher_notes: Keep safety and process evidence visible.

package_builder_template.title: Package Builder Template
package_builder_template.overview: Teacher-facing passoff template for turning one target card into an engine-ready package.
package_builder_template.project_prompt: Create package title, grade, subject, context family, target, student-friendly goal, product, evidence, artifacts, and separation notes.
materials: teacher answer key only if needed
package_builder_template.assessment_focus: The passoff protects target alignment, observable evidence, and teacher/student separation.
teacher_notes: Use plain Grade 8 language and low-clutter outputs.

artifact_checklist.title: Artifact Checklist
artifact_checklist.project_prompt: Check curriculum target card, student packet, teacher guide, K-support, rubric/checklist, exit tickets, and teacher-only answer key if needed.
artifact_checklist.assessment_focus: Every artifact needs a clear role and no answer leakage into student-facing materials.
teacher_notes: Use before generating fixtures and after rendering.

teacher_context_guidance.title: Teacher Context Guidance
teacher_context_guidance.overview: Teacher-facing guidance for choosing context families.
teacher_context_guidance.project_prompt: Start with curriculum target. Choose context only after target is clear.
materials: Use one main context family unless intentionally cross-curricular.
Use Kamloops/local context when it increases relevance without requiring extra research.
Use short practical contexts for direct skill-building packages.
teacher_context_guidance.assessment_focus: Context must fit the target and not add unnecessary reading load.
teacher_notes: Use protocol-aware language and teacher-selected/local-approved sources for First Peoples content.

student_support_frame_template.title: Student Support Frame Template
student_support_frame_template.purpose_line: Reusable reduced-load support frame. This page is student-facing.
instructions: Name the learning goal, evidence, context, words, and starting point.

tasks.label: reference bank
tasks.prompt: Words I need and support tools I can use.

tasks.label: research planner
tasks.prompt: I can show this by creating this product or evidence.

tasks.label: My context
tasks.prompt: The context I am using is...

tasks.label: completion_check
tasks.prompt: I named the learning goal. I completed the required product or evidence. I used support tools. I completed the checklist or asked for help.

embedded_supports: Sentence frame: I am learning to ___.
Sentence frame: I can show this by ___.
success_criteria: done-when: I understand the learning goal.
done-when: I produced the required evidence.

student_context_choice_frame.title: Student Context Choice Frame
student_context_choice_frame.purpose_line: Reusable student context-choice frame. This page is student-facing.
instructions: Choose one context that helps you understand the task.
Your context should still match the same learning goal.

tasks.label: Before choosing
tasks.prompt: Your teacher may assign the context if choice would make the task too confusing.

tasks.label: reference bank
tasks.prompt: Garden / Planter / Food Systems; Design / Build / Trades; Community / Kamloops / Local Work; Outdoor Place / Land / Environment; Health / Body / Choices; Digital / Coding / Data; Consumer / Money / Market; Stories / Identity / Growth

tasks.label: research planner
tasks.prompt: I chose this context because it helps me understand the same learning goal.

tasks.label: completion_check
tasks.prompt: I chose a context that matches the learning goal. I can explain why it fits. I know what product or evidence I need to create.

embedded_supports: The context should help you understand the same learning goal, not change the task.
success_criteria: done-when: I chose a context that matches the learning goal.
done-when: I can explain why it fits.
done-when: I know what product or evidence I need to create.
